flight_prices_twd.description: Estimated monthly average round-trip flight prices TPE-FUK in TWD. Based on Skyscanner, Expedia, KAYAK data: cheapest month is September (~NT$7,000 RT), most expensive is April (~NT$14,000 RT during cherry blossom). Budget carriers (Peach, Tiger) start ~NT$3,400 one-way; full-service (EVA, JAL, STARLUX) ~NT$6,700-8,400 one-way.
flight_prices_twd.note: Prices are estimated averages combining LCC and full-service carriers for round-trip bookings. Actual prices vary by booking lead time and availability.

monthly_averages_round_trip.notes: Post-New Year lull, good deals available
Lunar New Year spike mid-month, otherwise moderate
Cherry blossom demand drives prices up late March
Peak cherry blossom + spring break; most expensive month
Golden Week (Apr 29-May 6) inflates early May prices
Summer holidays begin; Yamakasa festival draws visitors
Obon holiday period (mid-Aug) drives prices up
Typhoon season + post-summer; cheapest month overall

festivals_and_events.name: Tamaseseri (玉取祭 / Ball-Catching Festival)
festivals_and_events.description: Men compete to seize a sacred wooden ball at Hakozaki Shrine; one of Kyushu's three great festivals

festivals_and_events.name: Toka Ebisu (十日恵比須祭)
festivals_and_events.name_jp: 十日恵比須神社正月大祭
festivals_and_events.description: Four-day festival at Toka Ebisu Shrine praying for business prosperity and good fortune

festivals_and_events.name: Setsubun Festivals
festivals_and_events.description: Bean-throwing ceremonies at temples and shrines to drive away evil spirits and welcome spring

festivals_and_events.name: Yanagawa Sagemon Festival
festivals_and_events.description: Colorful hanging ornaments displayed throughout Yanagawa city for Girls' Day (Hina Matsuri)

festivals_and_events.name: Ume (Plum Blossom) Viewing
festivals_and_events.description: Plum blossoms at Dazaifu Tenmangu Shrine and Maizuru Park; 6,000 trees at Dazaifu

festivals_and_events.name: Cherry Blossom Season (Hanami)
festivals_and_events.description: Fukuoka blooms among Japan's earliest; top spots: Maizuru Park (1,000 trees), Nishi Park, Atago Shrine

festivals_and_events.name: Fukuoka Castle Sakura Festival
festivals_and_events.date_range: Late March - early April (approx. Mar 25 - Apr 6)
festivals_and_events.description: Night illumination of cherry blossoms at Maizuru Park/Fukuoka Castle ruins with food stalls

festivals_and_events.name: National Umeshu Festival
festivals_and_events.description: Taste and buy plum wines from across Japan at JR Hakata Station Square

festivals_and_events.name: Wisteria Viewing at Nakayama Kumano Shrine
festivals_and_events.description: 300-year-old giant wisteria trellis in Yame City; one of Fukuoka's top spring sights

festivals_and_events.name: Hakata Dontaku Port Festival
festivals_and_events.name_jp: 博多どんたく港まつり
festivals_and_events.description: Japan's largest festival by attendance (~2.1 million visitors); parades, street performances, shamoji dancing

festivals_and_events.name: Hakata Gion Yamakasa
festivals_and_events.description: UNESCO-listed festival; men race carrying 1-ton decorated floats (kakiyama); climax Oiyama on July 15 at dawn

festivals_and_events.name: Fireworks Festivals
festivals_and_events.description: Multiple fireworks displays along rivers and coastline including Ohori Park fireworks

festivals_and_events.name: Kokura Gion Daiko
festivals_and_events.description: 400-year-old drum festival in Kitakyushu with unique two-sided drumming style

festivals_and_events.name: Tobata Gion Oyamagasa
festivals_and_events.description: Pyramid-shaped floats lit with hundreds of lanterns paraded through Tobata at night

festivals_and_events.name: Hojoya Festival
festivals_and_events.description: Autumn festival at Hakozaki Shrine with ~500 food/game stalls lining the approach; 1,000+ year history

festivals_and_events.name: Asian Month / Hakata Akihaku
festivals_and_events.name_jp: アジアマンス・博多秋博
festivals_and_events.description: City-wide cultural events celebrating Fukuoka's connections to Asia; food, art, performances

festivals_and_events.name: Autumn Foliage Season
festivals_and_events.description: Peak koyo at Nishi Park (Momiji Valley), Raizan Sennyoji, Kamado Shrine, Dazaifu Tenmangu

festivals_and_events.name: Grand Sumo Tournament (Kyushu Basho)
festivals_and_events.description: Annual sumo tournament at Fukuoka Kokusai Center; only Kyushu-based Grand Sumo event

festivals_and_events.name: Christmas Illuminations
festivals_and_events.name_jp: クリスマスイルミネーション
festivals_and_events.description: Light displays at Hakata Station, Tenjin, Canal City; holiday markets and seasonal food

festivals_and_events.name: Hakata Ramen Festival / Yatai Culture
festivals_and_events.name_jp: 博多ラーメン・屋台文化
festivals_and_events.description: Fukuoka's iconic outdoor food stalls (yatai) along Nakasu River serve tonkotsu ramen and more nightly

special_holiday_periods_2026.name: New Year (Oshogatsu)
special_holiday_periods_2026.note: Many businesses closed; temples crowded for hatsumode. Avoid unless you want the cultural experience.

special_holiday_periods_2026.name: Golden Week
special_holiday_periods_2026.note: Showa Day (Wed Apr 29) + Constitution Memorial Day (Sun May 3) through observed holiday (Wed May 6). Extremely crowded; flights and hotels spike 30-50%.

special_holiday_periods_2026.name: Obon
special_holiday_periods_2026.note: Not an official holiday but most workers take leave. Domestic travel surge; Fukuoka less affected than rural areas.

special_holiday_periods_2026.name: Silver Week
special_holiday_periods_2026.note: RARE 5-day holiday (first since 2015, next not until 2032). Sat 19 + Sun 20 + Respect for Aged Day Mon 21 + Citizens' Holiday Tue 22 + Autumn Equinox Wed 23. Expect significant crowds.

highlights: Cherry blossoms late March - early April (Fukuoka blooms among Japan's earliest)
Hakata Dontaku (May 3-4) - Japan's biggest festival by attendance
drawbacks: Hotels book up for hanami season

highlights: Hakata Gion Yamakasa (Jul 1-15) - UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage
November: autumn foliage peaks mid-Nov to early Dec
drawbacks: Silver Week 2026 will be unusually crowded

highlights: Hot springs (onsen) season; easy access to Beppu/Yufuin
Fewer crowds; great for food-focused trips
Fukuoka winters are mild compared to Tokyo/Osaka

seasonal_guide.best_overall_recommendation: Late October to mid-November: perfect weather, autumn colors beginning, Sumo tournament, reasonable flight prices, manageable crowds
